pattern: A Fenwick (binary indexed) tree supports both point updates and prefix-sum queries in logarithmic time, so range sums stay fast even as values change.
hints: A plain prefix-sum array answers queries fast but every update forces rebuilding a whole suffix, so neither operation can be slow on its own.
You want a structure where updating one position and summing a prefix both cost the same modest amount of work.

A binary indexed tree stores partial sums at positions keyed by their lowest set bit; an update walks upward adding the delta, a prefix query walks downward accumulating, and a range sum is the difference of two prefix queries.

edge_inputs.description: the statement's own sequence: query, update, query again
single-element array, update then read it back
updates must not double-count: setting the same index twice replaces, not adds
partial range that excludes an updated index stays unchanged
negatives and a full-range sum after several updates

stress.description: random array with interleaved updates and valid range queries
stress.note: left<=right enforced by construction; updates set absolute values like the real API

complexity.time: O(log n) per update and query
